rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAUREEN E. CROWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition caption indicates that this is for preliminary distribution, but the entire estate is proposed to be distributed. (2) A petition for distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain). [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (3) The petition does not address the appraised value of the estate, creditor's claims, or how the statutory compensation was calculated. [CRC 7.550(b). (4) If the statutory compensation is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] The Corrected I&A filed on 2/21/24 showed the value of the estate to be $474,198.87, which would mean the statutory compensation would be $12,483.98. (5) The report does not address the disclosures required to be made, pursuant to Probate Code Section 9202, et seq., and LRSF 14.35 I. (6) The proof of service filed on 9/16/24 indicates that the notice was mailed on 9/13/24. The amended petition was not filed until 9/16/24 or e-filed until 9/17/24. [LRSF 14.14.] (7) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (and amendment to the amended petition) to the assigned examiner by 12/2/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Personal representatives are not discharged at the time of final distribution; an ex parte petition must be filed with receipts attached or after receipts are filed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF QIAO E. MAI CHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/30/2024): 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing, absent a waiver signed by each person entitled to notice. 3) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 4) All allegations required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) must be made, including: i) Has notice been given to all known or reasonably ascertainable creditors of the decedent, as required by Probate Code section 9050? ii) Has any person requested special notice? iii) Are there any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution, other than the assignment signed by the decedent's sibling? 5) The petition requested an extension of time to administer the estate for 2 months until 09/12/2024. If additional time is still necessary, the petition must be amended and renoticed. 6) If the predeceased spouse's 1/18 interest in the Union St. property that passed to the decedent was the predeceased spouse's separate property in its entirety, should the predeceased spouse's issue take the decedent's 1/18 interest directly under section 6402.5(a)(1) and (f)(4), rather than as assignees of the decedent's sibling (who may not be entitled to succeed to the decedent's 1/18 interest)? Note: An appearance may be required on 12/16/2024 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, unless papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are filed.

(2) No notice was given of the amendment e-filed on 10/21/24. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) Page 2 of the petition was incomplete (cut off). (5) Did the grantor leave a written memorandum disposing of tangible personal property, as mentioned in Section 6.1 of Article 6? Please provide a courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/9/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TINA A HAYS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/25/2024. The following issues need to be cleared two weeks prior to the next hearing: (1) Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access pursuant to Local Rule 14.1.C.6). (1) Petition conclusively alleges that the funds in the bank account subject to this petition are community property while the account is in the decedent's name only. Petitioner must file a verified supplement to present the information required by the court pursuant to Local Rule 14.26.A. (2) Proof of service of the above-mentioned supplement to all persons who are entitled to notice must be filed. CRC 7.53. (3) When second-generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. Attachment 9 lists several nephews and a niece; however, their (deceased) ancestors are not listed. (4) Attachments 7a and 7b need to specify the share (percentage) each of 7a and 7b is sought different statutes. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
